summary: Add a transport-neutral MCP JSON-RPC dispatcher that validates tools, returns protocol errors, holds denied mutations as pending_review, and delegates allowed calls to an application handler.

decisions.decision: Keep JSON-RPC dispatch independent of stdio and desktop process code; validate tools through mcp-tools, return -32601/-32602 protocol errors, hold denied mutations as pending_review, and delegate only permitted calls.
decisions.rationale: This creates a testable command-bus seam without inventing transport lifecycle behavior before the desktop stdio integration is designed and gated.

testEvidence.criterion: The dispatcher tests pass for tools/list, unknown-method protocol errors, denied mutation review status, and allowed handler delegation.
testEvidence.test: Run the focused MCP contract and dispatcher suites once after implementation.

red.outcome: RED: the dispatcher module and its handler contract were not yet present before this unit began.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 2 passed (2); Tests 5 passed (5); Duration 438ms.

testEvidence.criterion: The dispatcher remains type-safe and source-policy clean.
testEvidence.test: Run TypeScript and repository lint/source policy once after implementation.

red.outcome: RED: the dispatcher module was not yet present before this unit began.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false; pnpm.cmd lint
green.outcome: GREEN: TypeScript exited 0; Checked 435 files in 209ms with source policy passed.

questions: The dispatcher currently returns an empty tools/list and has no stdio framing, initialize handshake, desktop handler registry, or process-off proof; AC-11.b/c remain open.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
